coffees.name: Yirgacheffe
coffees.history: One of the world's most distinctive coffees from the birthplace of coffee. Yirgacheffe is known for its floral, tea-like qualities.

coffees.name: Sidamo
coffees.history: From the Sidamo highlands, producing coffees with complex berry notes and wine-like characteristics.

coffees.name: Harrar
coffees.tasting_notes: Wild blueberry, dark chocolate, wine, apricot
coffees.history: Dry-processed Ethiopian coffee with distinctive blueberry and wine notes, one of the oldest coffee-growing regions.

coffees.name: Colombian Supremo
coffees.harvest_season: September to December, April to June
coffees.history: Colombian Supremo refers to the largest bean size. Known for balanced, mild flavor and consistent quality.

coffees.name: Huila
coffees.harvest_season: April to June, October to December
coffees.tasting_notes: Caramel, orange, red apple, cane sugar
coffees.history: Southern Colombian region producing some of the country's finest coffees with bright acidity and sweet caramel notes.

coffees.name: Nariño
coffees.tasting_notes: Orange, honey, floral, apricot, brown sugar
coffees.history: High-altitude Colombian region near Ecuador producing exceptionally bright, complex coffees.

coffees.name: Kenya AA
coffees.harvest_season: October to December, May to July
coffees.tasting_notes: Blackcurrant, grapefruit, wine, tomato, brown sugar
coffees.history: AA grade refers to the largest bean size. Kenyan coffee is known for intense brightness and distinctive blackcurrant notes.

coffees.name: Tarrazú
coffees.history: From the slopes of the Poas and Barva volcanoes, known for clean, bright profile with chocolate notes.

coffees.name: Antigua
coffees.history: Grown in volcanic soil near three volcanoes, produces coffee with distinctive spicy, smoky chocolate notes.

coffees.name: Huehuetenango
coffees.tasting_notes: Red fruit, wine, chocolate, floral, citrus
coffees.history: Highest and most remote region in Guatemala, protected from frost and producing exceptional quality.

coffees.name: Santos
coffees.history: Brazil's most famous coffee export, known for chocolate and nutty notes with low acidity. Ideal for espresso.

coffees.name: Cerrado
coffees.history: First Brazilian region to receive PDO status, known for consistent quality and sweet, chocolatey profile.

coffees.name: Jamaica Blue Mountain
coffees.history: One of the world's most expensive coffees, grown in the Blue Mountains. Exceptionally smooth and mild with no bitterness.

coffees.name: Kona
coffees.tasting_notes: Nuts, brown sugar, chocolate, mild fruit
coffees.history: Grown on the volcanic slopes of Mauna Loa. One of the most expensive coffees due to high labor costs and limited supply.

coffees.name: Sumatra Mandheling
coffees.flavor_profile: Earthy, herbal, low acidity, full body
coffees.harvest_season: September to December, June to July
coffees.tasting_notes: Earth, cedar, herbs, dark chocolate, tobacco
coffees.history: Unique wet-hulled processing creates distinctive earthy, full-bodied profile. Named after Mandailing people.

coffees.name: Java
coffees.history: One of the original coffee islands, giving its name to the slang term for coffee. Rich and full-bodied.

coffees.name: Sulawesi Toraja
coffees.flavor_profile: Earthy, herbal, dark fruit, full body
coffees.history: Grown in the Toraja highlands, known for full body and complex earthy flavors.

coffees.name: Vietnamese Robusta
coffees.history: Vietnam is the world's largest Robusta producer. Strong, bitter, ideal for Vietnamese iced coffee with condensed milk.

coffees.name: Geisha (Panama)
coffees.tasting_notes: Jasmine, bergamot, tropical fruit, peach, mango
coffees.history: One of the world's most expensive and sought-after coffees. Extraordinary floral complexity, often scoring 90+.

coffees.name: Rwanda Bourbon
coffees.history: Rwandan coffee has rapidly gained recognition for quality. Bourbon variety produces bright, clean cups.

coffees.name: Burundi Bourbon
coffees.tasting_notes: Red fruit, citrus, floral, brown sugar
coffees.history: Neighbor to Rwanda with similar terroir. Produces bright, fruity coffees with excellent acidity.

coffees.name: Tanzania Peaberry
coffees.flavor_profile: Wine-like, citrus, black currant, full body
coffees.history: Peaberry is a natural mutation (single round bean). Similar to Kenyan coffee with wine-like characteristics.

coffees.name: Matagalpa
coffees.history: Northern Nicaraguan region producing balanced, chocolatey coffees with good body.

coffees.name: Chiapas
coffees.history: Southern Mexican coffee region near Guatemala, producing mild, chocolatey coffees.

coffees.name: Chanchamayo
coffees.history: Central Peruvian region producing balanced, chocolatey coffees often grown organically.

coffees.name: Marcala
coffees.history: First Honduran region to receive PDO status. Produces balanced, sweet coffees.

coffees.name: Pacamara (El Salvador)
coffees.history: Pacamara is a hybrid of Pacas and Maragogipe. Large beans with complex, fruity profile.

coffees.name: Yemen Mocha
coffees.tasting_notes: Wine, chocolate, spice, wild berry, earth
coffees.history: Birthplace of commercial coffee cultivation. Mocha (Al Mukha) was the port of export. Complex, wild, wine-like.

coffees.name: Monsooned Malabar
coffees.history: Unique monsoon processing exposes beans to monsoon winds, creating distinctive low-acid, heavy-bodied profile.

regions.name: Sidamo/Gedeo
regions.description: Birthplace of Arabica coffee, home to Yirgacheffe

regions.name: Antigua Valley
regions.description: Surrounded by three volcanoes, unique microclimate

regions.name: Tarrazú
regions.description: Famous Costa Rican region, Poas and Barva volcanoes

regions.name: Central Highlands (Kenya)
regions.description: Kiambu, Nyeri, Kirinyaga counties, known for brightness

regions.name: Kona District
regions.description: Big Island slopes of Mauna Loa and Hualalai

regions.name: Blue Mountain
regions.description: Strict geographical designation, produces world's most expensive coffee

regions.name: Minas Gerais
regions.description: Largest coffee producing state in Brazil

regions.name: Western Highlands
regions.description: Organic by default, similar profile to Indonesian coffees

regions.name: Kilimanjaro/Arusha
regions.description: Slopes of Mt. Kilimanjaro and Mt. Meru

regions.name: Haraz Mountains
regions.description: Birthplace of commercial coffee, ancient terraced farms

varieties.name: Typica
varieties.characteristics: Original Arabica variety, tall plants, low yield, excellent quality
varieties.notes: Foundation variety from which many others derive

varieties.name: Bourbon
varieties.characteristics: Natural mutation of Typica, higher yield, excellent quality
varieties.notes: Named after Bourbon Island (Réunion), basis for many quality coffees

varieties.name: Geisha/Gesha
varieties.characteristics: Discovered in Ethiopia, popularized in Panama, extraordinary quality
varieties.notes: Can score 90+ in competitions, fetches record auction prices

varieties.name: SL28
varieties.characteristics: Selected by Scott Labs in Kenya, drought tolerant, exceptional quality
varieties.notes: One of Kenya's signature varieties, distinctive blackcurrant notes

varieties.name: SL34
varieties.characteristics: Selected by Scott Labs, excellent in high rainfall areas
varieties.notes: Complementary to SL28 in Kenyan blends

varieties.name: Caturra
varieties.characteristics: Natural mutation of Bourbon, compact size, good yield
varieties.notes: Popular in Latin America, easier to harvest due to compact size

varieties.name: Catuai
varieties.characteristics: Hybrid of Mundo Novo and Caturra, compact, productive
varieties.notes: Popular commercial variety, stable cherry attachment

varieties.name: Mundo Novo
varieties.characteristics: Natural hybrid of Typica and Bourbon, vigorous, high yield
varieties.notes: Important Brazilian variety, foundation for many hybrids

varieties.name: Pacamara
varieties.characteristics: Hybrid of Pacas and Maragogipe, large beans, complex
varieties.notes: Large bean size, capable of exceptional quality

varieties.name: Castillo
varieties.characteristics: Developed for disease resistance, maintains quality
varieties.notes: Modern variety replacing traditional Colombian types

varieties.name: Timor Hybrid
varieties.characteristics: Natural hybrid with Robusta genes, disease resistant
varieties.notes: Foundation for many modern disease-resistant varieties

varieties.name: Catimor
varieties.characteristics: Cross of Caturra and Timor Hybrid, disease resistant
varieties.notes: High yield but sometimes compromised quality

varieties.name: Ruiru 11
varieties.characteristics: Disease resistant variety developed in Kenya
varieties.notes: CBD and rust resistant, compact plant

varieties.name: Maragogipe
varieties.characteristics: Natural mutation of Typica, very large beans
varieties.notes: Elephant bean, largest coffee bean, mild flavor

varieties.name: Pacas
varieties.characteristics: Natural mutation of Bourbon, compact size
varieties.notes: Important in El Salvador coffee industry

varieties.name: Kent
varieties.characteristics: Selected for disease resistance in India
varieties.notes: Historical importance in Indian coffee

varieties.name: S795
varieties.characteristics: Cross of Kent and S228, disease resistant
varieties.notes: Popular in India and Indonesia

varieties.name: Villa Sarchi
varieties.characteristics: Natural mutation of Bourbon, compact
varieties.notes: Boutique variety in Central America

varieties.name: Colombia
varieties.characteristics: Cross involving Caturra, disease resistant
varieties.notes: Widely planted in Colombia, replacing traditional varieties

varieties.name: Robusta (Coffea canephora)
varieties.characteristics: Separate species, hardy, high caffeine, lower quality
varieties.notes: 40% of world production, used in espresso blends and instant coffee

brewing_methods.name: Espresso
brewing_methods.description: High pressure extraction creating concentrated shot with crema. Foundation for cappuccino, latte, etc.

brewing_methods.name: Pour Over (V60)
brewing_methods.flavor_characteristics: Clean, bright, highlights acidity and complexity
brewing_methods.description: Japanese cone-shaped dripper with spiral ribs. Allows for precise control and clean extraction.

brewing_methods.name: Chemex
brewing_methods.equipment_needed: Chemex brewer, Chemex filters, kettle, scale
brewing_methods.description: Hourglass-shaped glass brewer with thick proprietary filters, produces exceptionally clean cup.

brewing_methods.name: French Press
brewing_methods.flavor_characteristics: Full body, rich, textured, some sediment
brewing_methods.description: Full immersion brewing with metal mesh filter. Allows oils and fine particles through for rich body.

brewing_methods.name: AeroPress
brewing_methods.flavor_characteristics: Versatile, clean to full body depending on method
brewing_methods.description: Portable brewer using air pressure. Extremely versatile with multiple brewing methods possible.

brewing_methods.name: Moka Pot
brewing_methods.description: Stovetop brewer popular in Italy. Creates strong coffee through steam pressure, not true espresso.

brewing_methods.name: Cold Brew
brewing_methods.description: Long steep in cold water extracts different compounds, resulting in smooth, low-acid concentrate.

brewing_methods.name: Siphon/Vacuum Pot
brewing_methods.description: Uses vapor pressure and vacuum to brew. Theatrical and produces clean, complex cup.

brewing_methods.name: Turkish/Ibrik
brewing_methods.description: Ancient method boiling ultra-fine coffee with sugar. Traditional in Middle East and Eastern Europe.

brewing_methods.name: Drip Coffee Maker
brewing_methods.flavor_characteristics: Varies by machine, generally clean and balanced
brewing_methods.description: Automatic electric brewer. Convenient but quality varies significantly by machine.

brewing_methods.name: Clever Dripper
brewing_methods.flavor_characteristics: Clean but full-bodied, best of both methods
brewing_methods.description: Combines immersion brewing with paper filter. Easier than pour over, cleaner than French press.

brewing_methods.name: Vietnamese Phin
brewing_methods.description: Small metal filter that slowly drips concentrated coffee. Traditionally served with condensed milk.

roasters.description: World's largest coffee chain, Seattle-based, known for dark roasts and espresso drinks
Major US coffee and donut chain, emphasis on drip coffee and value
Founded by Alfred Peet, inspired Starbucks founders, known for dark roasts
Italy's favorite coffee, family-owned, pioneer in blending and sustainability
Premium Italian brand, single blend philosophy, pressurized packaging innovation
World's largest instant coffee brand, Nestlé's coffee division
America's best-selling coffee brand, known for slogan 'The Best Part of Waking Up'
Third-wave pioneer, emphasis on freshness and single-origin, acquired by Nestlé 2017
Third-wave pioneer, Direct Trade program, multiple US locations
Specialty pioneer, transparency, sustainability, B Corp certified since 2009
Portland-based third-wave pioneer, direct trade relationships, cold brew innovation
East Coast specialty leader, draft latte innovation, multiple cafes
California-based specialty roaster, multiple locations, emphasis on direct relationships
World Barista Champion, micro-roaster, direct sourcing, education focused
Founded by James Hoffmann (World Barista Champion), focus on quality and education

roasters.certifications: Direct Trade, In My Mug program
roasters.description: UK specialty pioneer, subscription model innovation, transparency
Scandinavian specialty leader, micro-lots, Nordic roasting style
Copenhagen-based, democratic ownership, World Barista Championship titles
Tokyo specialty leader, multiple locations, Japanese precision
Australian specialty pioneer, roasting education, multiple state presence
Sydney-based, large Australian presence, cafe culture leader
New Zealand heritage, international expansion, consistent quality
America's original whole bean coffee, value-focused, grocery staple
Historic American brand, 'Good to the Last Drop' slogan since 1915
Premium instant coffee line, single-serve packets
Cuban-style coffee, strong espresso-style blend, Latino market leader
Italian-American espresso brand, New York heritage
World's strongest coffee claim, high caffeine, online-focused brand
